Badger Grapple Red v16.0 — GBA ROM-Hack Shell

Purpose:
- Stop chasing a full ROM hack while phone-only remains the constraint.
- Make the web game present like a GBA ROM-hack build: fixed 240x160 internal canvas, pixel-perfect scaling, denser UI, smaller sprites, and less prototype-like screen composition.

Changes:
- Phaser canvas changed from 480x432 to true GBA-style 240x160.
- CSS adds a handheld/bezel shell and forces pixelated/crisp scaling.
- Overworld tile size changed from 32px to 16px.
- Fieldhouse scene regenerated at 448x224 using 16px tiles.
- Player/NPC sprites regenerated as 16x24 frames.
- Title background regenerated at 240x160.
- Battle background regenerated at 240x160.
- Title, starter, scout, overworld, battle, and menu scenes rewritten for the smaller internal resolution.
- HUD/dialogue/menu boxes now use square, ROM-like borders instead of large rounded web UI.
- On-screen controls relabeled to Start/A/B style.
- Version/cache bumped to v16.0 / ?v=160.

Phone test:
- Deploy the root contents or dist folder.
- Open the Pages URL once with ?v=160.
- Confirm the game viewport is 3:2 and looks like a scaled GBA screen, not a large web canvas.

Checks:
- JS syntax checks passed for all src modules.
- Package root contains .github workflow and dist mirror.
